audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Elihu's final storm speech: trembling heart, thunder/voice, snow/rain, sealed human hand, animals, cloud balance, heat/wind, human inability to arrange speech, and awe before Shaddai. Serves as transition into the whirlwind.

verses.restored: At this also my heart trembles and leaps from its place.
Listen, listen to the roar of his voice and the rumbling that goes out from his mouth.
Under all the heavens he lets it loose, and his lightning to the corners of the earth.
After it a voice roars; he thunders with his majestic voice, and he does not hold them back when his voice is heard.
The Divine thunders with his voice wondrously, doing great things that we do not know.
For to the snow he says, Fall on the earth, and to the shower of rain and the heavy shower of his strength.
He seals the hand of every human, so that all people may know his work.
The beast goes into its lair and remains in its dens.
From the chamber comes the storm, and cold from the scattering winds.
By the breath of the Divine ice is given, and the breadth of waters is constrained.
Indeed, he loads the thick cloud with moisture; he scatters the cloud of his lightning.
It turns around by his guidance, to do all that he commands upon the face of the inhabited earth.
Whether for rod, or for his land, or for covenant-kindness, he causes it to happen.
Give ear to this, Job; stand still and consider the wonders of the Divine.
Do you know how the Divine lays command upon them and makes the light of his cloud shine?
Do you know the balancings of the clouds, the wonders of one complete in knowledge?
You whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind,
can you, with him, spread out the skies, strong as a cast metal mirror?
Make known to us what we should say to him; we cannot arrange words because of darkness.
Should it be told him that I would speak? If a man said so, surely he would be swallowed up.
Now people do not see the light, bright as it is in the skies, when wind passes and clears them.
From the north comes gold-like brightness; around the Divine is fearsome majesty.
Shaddai, we cannot find him out; he is great in power and justice, and abundant in righteousness; he does not oppress.
Therefore people fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in heart.
